commit.message:
ci: remove 9 redundant workflows superseded by new 5-stage pipeline

Removed:
- ci-cd-pipeline.yml (monolithic pipeline, replaced by CI1→CD3 chain)
- deploy-prod.yml (bypassed approval gates)
- deploy.yml (duplicate of CI1, caused race conditions)
- deploy-aks.yml (now handled by CD1/CD3)
- deploy-cimigrate.yml (duplicated CD1 upgrade tests)
- deploy-cireset.yml (duplicated CD1 setup tests)
- performance-tests.yml (duplicated CD1 perf tests)
- release-notes.yml (now handled by CD2)
- reset-data-staging.yml (duplicated CD1 setup tests)

Remaining workflows:
- unit-tests.yml (CI1), container.yml (CI2), cd-canary.yml (CD1),
  cd-early-adopters.yml (CD2), cd-full-rollout.yml (CD3)
- build-cli.yml, codeql.yml, deploy-tenant.yml (reusable),
  download-static-assets.yml, perf-large.yml (nightly)

commit.message:
fix: add triggers to time_sleep so it re-fires on DC replacement

Without triggers, time_sleep.wait_for_dc_reboot is not re-created when
the EC2 instance is replaced, causing vault_ldap_secrets to start before
the DC is ready. Keying on instance_id ensures the wait re-fires.

* Fix MentionInput parser regression

Repair the malformed merge artifact in MentionInput that was breaking frontend lint in PR 2651.

The failing GitHub Actions run reported a parser error in frontend/src/components/chat/MentionInput.tsx. The file had duplicated fragments from two versions of the component: an imperative handle block had been spliced into the mount effect, the keydown callback was missing its closing brace before the dependency array, and the placeholder JSX tail contained both the newer responsive placeholder path and an older duplicate fallback block.

This change removes the duplicated fragments and restores the intended component structure without altering runtime behavior. The final file keeps the existing responsive placeholder support, cycling placeholder rendering, and imperative handle API, but returns to a syntactically valid single implementation.

responsibilities including core proxy routing, HTTP header manipulation,
functional options configuration, and image format detection.

tests, and deviates from the Go idiom of keeping small, focused files
with a strict 1:1 mapping between implementation (`.go`) and test files

> Refactor the monolithic `manael.go` and `manael_test.go` by splitting

task; no business logic or external APIs will be changed. This
reorganization will significantly improve code readability and make
future contributions easier.

Merging changes from OKOK to main (#22)

* Set CAN ids and pneumatic channels, tested intake feed, hopper feed, and turret uptake. Intake feed and uptake have correct inversion and max speeds now, but a gearbox is needed for the hopper feed. Flywheel motors will need to be changed to be spark flex instead of TalonFX

Added automatic detection of when the climb has reversed direction (the motor has gone too far) once the climber hits the limit switch again when going a direction where it shouldn't be able to hit the limit switch (commanded to go up, but hits the limit switch). This reverses the direction of inputs to maintain functionality and is utilized to stop the climber from over rotating and winding down into itself. commit.message:
fix: update stale client reference after reconnect in SendPromptAsync (#325)

## Bug
Multi-agent orchestrator sessions (e.g. "PR Review Squad-orchestrator")
fail to recover after connection drops, showing:
```
⚠️ Session disconnected and reconnect failed: Client not connected. Call StartAsync() first.
```

## Root Cause
In `SendPromptAsync`'s reconnect path, a local `client` variable is
captured from `GetClientForGroup()` **before** the client is recreated.
After the code disposes the old `_client` and creates a new one (lines
2408-2413), the local `client` still points to the **old disposed**
`CopilotClient`. The subsequent `client.ResumeSessionAsync()` /
`client.CreateSessionAsync()` calls use the stale reference, throwing
`InvalidOperationException: Client not connected. Call StartAsync()
first.`

```
Line 2385:  var client = GetClientForGroup(...)  // captures OLD _client
Line 2408:  await _client.DisposeAsync()          // disposes old
Line 2411:  _client = CreateClient(...)           // creates NEW _client  
Line 2412:  await _client.StartAsync(...)         // starts new
            // BUG: `client` still points to OLD disposed _client!
Line 2444:  await client.ResumeSessionAsync(...)  // uses STALE ref → crash
```

## Fix
One-line addition: `client = _client;` after successful client
recreation, so the resume/create calls use the new connected client.

## Tests
Added 5 regression tests in `ConnectionRecoveryTests.cs`:
- `GetClientForGroup_ReturnsCurrentClient_AfterReconnectClientIsNew`
- `IsConnectionError_DetectsOrchestratorDispatchError`
- `IsConnectionError_DetectsConnectionLostFollowedByNotConnected`
- `SendPromptAsync_DemoMode_ConnectionRecovery_SucceedsWithNewClient`

All 2,283 tests pass. Mac Catalyst build succeeds.

---------

fix: reorder noise filter after LLM review and preserve section boundaries

Two fixes to the sentence extraction pipeline:

1. Move _is_noise_sentence() from extraction loops (pre-LLM) to
   lazygraphrag_pipeline.py (post-LLM). The correct sequence is now:
   split → preamble join → LLM review → noise filter → embed.

   (\n\n) in _clean_chunk_text_for_spacy(). This preserves section
   boundaries so the sentence splitter doesn't merge text across
   sections. Also adds cleanup for colon-period artifacts (:.) that
   arise when colon-terminated preambles precede stripped headings.

commit.message:
docs: update SECURITY.md with supported versions and reporting guidelines (#1697)

With the v3.0 release, the existing one-liner security policy is
insufficient — v2.x.x will continue receiving security and critical bug
fix support, and reporters need clearer guidance.

### Changes
- **Supported Versions table**: explicitly marks `3.x.x` (active),
`2.x.x` (security/critical fixes only), and `< 2.0.0` (unsupported)
- **Reporting guidelines**: instructs reporters to use
`contact@manael.org` instead of public issues, and lists expected
information (version, repro steps, impact)

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Docs: Update SECURITY.md to include supported
versions</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> Currently, our `SECURITY.md` contains only a minimal sentence stating
that vulnerabilities are fixed only in the latest major version. With
the recent release of Manael v3.0, which introduces significant
architectural changes (like the `libvips` migration), it is important to
clearly define our support policy.
> To accommodate users who cannot immediately migrate, we will continue
to provide security updates and critical bug fixes for the `v2.x.x`
line.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Update `SECURITY.md` to include a "Supported Versions" table that
explicitly states the support status for `3.x.x`, `2.x.x`, and `<
2.0.0`. Additionally, expand the "Reporting a Vulnerability" section to
provide clearer instructions on what information to include when
emailing the maintainers.
> 
> ### Tasks
> - [ ] Overwrite `SECURITY.md` with the new policy detailing support
for both v3 and v2.
> - [ ] Add explicit guidelines for reporting vulnerabilities via
`contact@manael.org`.
> 
> ### Target Content (`SECURITY.md`)
> ```markdown
> # Security Policy
> 
> ## Supported Versions
> 
> Manael currently supports the following versions for security updates:
> 
> | Version | Supported          | Notes |
